Kaizer Charania Technical leader across staff and EM scope
About Projects
Connect

About

I build systems that scale, and I care about helping teams scale with them.

I operate across architecture, modernization, performance, mentoring, and execution discipline, with the goal of making both the system and the team more effective over time.

I build backend and platform systems, but the work that feels most representative of me usually sits a layer deeper than feature delivery. I am most useful when a system is under strain, a team is carrying too much friction, or the architecture needs to evolve without slowing the business down.

A lot of my experience has come from working on scheduling, routing, platform modernization, data pipelines, and internal engineering systems. I enjoy breaking those problems down, finding where the real drag lives, and rebuilding them in ways that improve performance, reliability, and delivery at the same time.

I tend to operate in the overlap between technical depth and execution leadership. I like staying close to architecture and system design, but I also care about review quality, onboarding, mentoring, planning rhythm, and the day-to-day mechanisms that help a team move well.

Operating style

How I think about systems, teams, and technical decisions.

The pattern I keep coming back to is durable decision-making: solve the immediate problem, reduce future drag, and leave the team in a better operating position.

Operating principles

I try to make technical leadership feel concrete. That usually means solving the immediate system problem, but also improving the decision quality, team rhythm, and engineering habits around it so the same friction does not keep coming back.

01

Systems over heroics

The goal is durable execution: architecture, process, and team cadence that keep working even when things get messy.

02

Business-aware engineering

The best projects here are not just technically elegant. They clearly changed reliability, delivery speed, or commercial outcomes.

03

Practical platform thinking

Enablement matters: templates, GraphQL rollout, onboarding plans, review rigor, and other leverage points that improve how teams build.

04

Technical leadership with people follow-through

The best results come from combining technical direction with coaching, clearer expectations, and operating rhythms that let other engineers succeed.

Career arc

How the scope of my work has expanded over time.

Step 01

Backend and data platform depth

Early work emphasized APIs, ETL and data workflows, identity-resolution systems, throughput scaling, and reusable microservice templates.

Step 02

Platform ownership and modernization

The scope expanded into architecture transitions, GraphQL and Hasura enablement, performance work, and stronger system-level technical judgment.

Step 03

Leadership and operating systems

Roadmap ownership, mentoring, onboarding, quality standards, and execution cadence became part of the role, not separate management overhead.

Step 04

AI retrieval and applied experimentation

More recent work extends the platform story into RAG and retrieval-backed internal tools grounded in real use cases.

Team leadership

Where the work moves beyond individual technical contribution.

A meaningful part of my work has been creating better review quality, clearer standards, stronger onboarding, and more reliable delivery habits across a team.

Leadership snapshot

I care about leadership most when it becomes tangible in the team’s day-to-day experience: clearer decisions, better review quality, stronger onboarding, and execution habits that reduce friction instead of adding more process.

01

I led backend and platform work across an 8-engineer team scope, pairing architecture direction with day-to-day delivery accountability.

02

I introduced stronger design-review rigor, GraphQL and Hasura enablement, onboarding support, and engineering standards that made the team easier to scale.

03

I helped reduce rework from 15% to 4.97% by improving technical quality and execution discipline together instead of treating them as separate problems.

04

My review history consistently points to mentoring, High Potential recognition, and Exceptional Performer ratings.

Strengths

The technical foundations I keep coming back to.

The core of my work remains backend systems, platform design, architecture-led execution, and the ability to stay close to technical detail while operating at broader scope.

Where I add the most leverage

Backend depth, platform judgment, and execution systems that make teams easier to scale.

The throughline in my work is not just shipping backend systems. It is making those systems faster, more durable, and easier for a team to build on without slowing delivery down.

Backend systems

PythonFastAPI / FlaskREST API designGraphQL / HasuraPostgres / DynamoDB / RedisPerformance optimization

Platform and execution

MicroservicesEvent-driven architectureAWS / GCPDocker / KubernetesRoadmap ownershipArchitecture reviewMentorship / onboardingRAG pipelines / AWS Bedrock

Supporting full-stack range

ReactApollo integrationFull-stack delivery
